understanding.goal_understanding: The goal in each level was to guide the player to the goal gem tile marked `+` without landing on spike tiles (`u`/`v`), using at most 2 attempts and 50 turns per attempt, and to continue clearing levels until the game is won.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Each turn allowed either a horizontal move (MOVE_LEFT/MOVE_RIGHT) or a CLICK on a specific grid coordinate to remove clickable breakable blocks (`x`). After any horizontal move, gravity automatically carried the player in the current gravity direction until they landed on a solid/support tile (`o`/`m`/`w`), hit a hazard (spikes end the run), or reached the goal (`+`). CLICK actions did not move the player directly but could open or block fall paths by removing blocks. UNDO (when used) would revert the previous action to recover from mistakes.

understanding.goal_understanding: The goal in each level was to guide the player to the goal gem tile marked `+` without landing on spike tiles (`u`/`v`), using at most 2 attempts and 50 turns per attempt, and to continue clearing levels until the game is won.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Each turn allowed either a horizontal move (MOVE_LEFT/MOVE_RIGHT) or a CLICK on a specific grid coordinate to remove clickable breakable blocks (`x`). After any horizontal move, gravity automatically carried the player in the current gravity direction until they landed on a solid/support tile (`o`/`m`/`w`), hit a hazard (spikes end the run), or reached the goal (`+`). CLICK actions did not move the player directly but could open or block fall paths by removing blocks. UNDO (when used) would revert the previous action to recover from mistakes.
